seoTitle: Granville Harbour vs Grasstree Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Granville Harbour and Grasstree Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Granville Harbour vs Grasstree Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Granville Harbour and Grasstree Hill.
heading: Granville Harbour vs Grasstree Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Granville Harbour, Tasmania and Grasstree Hill, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Grasstree Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Granville Harbour (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Granville Harbour or Grasstree Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Granville Harbour has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Grasstree Hill scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Granville Harbour scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Grasstree Hill scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Granville Harbour or Grasstree Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Grasstree Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
